title: Project CI pipeline (lint, typecheck, tests) via GitHub Actions
description: Set up a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on every push/PR to validate the monorepo: install deps, lint, typecheck, and run tests across all packages.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow installs dependencies with pnpm using a cached store for speed
Workflow runs `pnpm -r run lint`, `pnpm -r run typecheck`, and `pnpm -r run test` as separate steps
A failing lint, typecheck, or test step fails the workflow run
Workflow uses a pinned Node.js version matching the project's engines requirement

steps.details: Create `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with `on: [push, pull_request]`, a single `build` job running on `ubuntu-latest`.

steps.description: Set up Node.js and pnpm with caching
steps.details: Use `actions/setup-node` with a pinned major version (e.g. `20`) and `pnpm/action-setup`. Enable pnpm store caching via `cache: 'pnpm'` in `setup-node`.

steps.description: Add lint, typecheck, and test steps
steps.details: Add three separate steps: `pnpm -r run lint`, `pnpm -r run typecheck`, `pnpm -r run test`, each as its own step so failures are individually identifiable in the Actions UI.
Introduce a temporary lint error locally, push to a test branch, and confirm the workflow run fails at the lint step specifically (not a generic failure). Revert the temporary error.

technicalNotes: Root `package.json` scripts for `lint`/`typecheck`/`test` (from TASK-2) must exist in every workspace package, even as no-ops, or `pnpm -r run` will fail on packages missing the script — use `pnpm -r --if-present run <script>` if some packages intentionally lack a script.
This CI validates the SystemBook project's own codebase — distinct from the example CI step documented in TASK-45, which is for teams consuming the connector in their own repos.
